title: RabbitMQ 4.3 Delayed Retries, Priorities, and Consumer Timeouts · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for rabbitmq 4.3 delayed retries, priorities, and consumer timeouts and requires the smallest defensible RabbitMQ 4.3 feature and fallback compatibility plan with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for RabbitMQ 4.3 feature and fallback compatibility plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence.
Compare native delayed retries with TTL and DLX cycles by rewrite cost, loss and duplicate windows, head-of-line effects, topology, and version support.
Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum 32 strict priority levels with bounded use, starvation evidence, prefetch interaction, and per-priority monitoring.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Delayed Retry Policy · changed case · Quiz Delayed Retry Policy
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz messaging team handles case 6002 in a consumer fleet under a shared dependency outage. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum retry, priority, and timeout behavior on version and valid budgets; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then make the broker confirmation disappear after the message may have committed. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum delayed-retry policies for returned or failed deliveries with explicit delay, eligibility, attempt, and compatibility evidence.

Which action demonstrates Delayed Retry Policy without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Delayed Retry Policy · changed case · Quiz Delayed Retry Policy.

For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Delayed Retry Policy · changed case · Quiz Delayed Retry Policy, keep Delayed Retry Policy connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feature Version Gate
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Feature Version Gate: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz messaging team handles case 3669 in a consumer fleet under a shared dependency outage. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate RabbitMQ 4.3 quorum retry, priority, and timeout behavior on version and valid budgets; record causal telemetry without message IDs as metric labels; then remove the dead-letter binding and expose the resulting retention risk. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate 4.3-only behavior on deployed broker, queue type, policy support, client behavior, migration state, and tested fallback.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feature Version Gate.
steps.why: Retrieving Feature Version Gate through the concrete RabbitMQ 4.3 feature and fallback compatibility plan case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For RabbitMQ and TypeScript Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feature Version Gate, keep Feature Version Gate connected to executable TypeScript messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Gate 4.3-only behavior on deployed broker, queue type, policy support, client behavior, migration state, and tested fallback.

Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
